text.title: Poetry Project Setup — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every python-poetry-setup application must obey.

rule.statement: Use PEP 621 [project] table as the source of truth for name, version, dependencies, requires-python. Use [tool.poetry] only for Poetry-specific extensions.
rule.rationale: PEP 621 is the canonical metadata location; staying in it keeps the project portable between Poetry, uv, hatch, pdm.

rule.statement: poetry.lock is committed to the repo. CI installs from the lockfile.
rule.rationale: Without a committed lockfile every install resolves differently; reproducibility is gone.

rule.statement: Use [tool.poetry.group.dev.dependencies] for dev-only deps. Never mix dev tools (pytest, ruff) into runtime dependencies.
rule.rationale: Runtime install must stay slim; mixing groups bloats production containers.

rule.statement: For libraries (publishable to PyPI): use lower-bound only constraints (>=2.0,<3) instead of caret (^2.0). For applications: caret is acceptable.
rule.rationale: Caret on libraries forces downstream users into narrow version ranges; lower-bound-only is permissive and idiomatic for libraries.

rule.statement: Set virtualenvs.in-project = true so .venv lives next to pyproject. IDEs and CI both discover it.
rule.rationale: Hidden venvs in ~/.cache/pypoetry break IDE auto-detection and confuse new contributors.
